reasoning: All 84 customer patterns can be expressed as values.yaml overrides with minor chart fixes. The most critical expressiveness gap (per-environment EFS access points) is solvable via the existing mergeEnvironment deep-merge mechanism — environments.{name}.volumes.shared.accessPoint — though this requires explicit documentation. The affinity 'preferred' type requires a small template addition (currently only boolean required/none). No customer requires structural differences that Helm cannot express: no custom init containers, no custom sidecars, no non-standard volume types. The complexity range (15-74 parameters, median 31) is squarely in Helm's target zone. cdk8s would offer no structural advantage for any discovered pattern.

unexpressiblePatterns.pattern: Preferred (soft) pod anti-affinity — current chart only supports required or none
unexpressiblePatterns.workaround: Add affinity.type: enum (none|preferred|required) to values.yaml and extend the statefulset.yaml template with the preferred anti-affinity block. Small template change, no structural issue.
unexpressiblePatterns.cdk8sAdvantage: None — cdk8s would implement the same logic with the same effort.

unexpressiblePatterns.pattern: Custom NetworkPolicy resources (custom policies beyond the 4 standard templates)
customers: Minority — customers with custom network policies in namespace/network-policies/
unexpressiblePatterns.workaround: Deploy custom policies as separate kubectl manifests or via a wrapper Helm chart. Not a blocker for the dotCMS chart scope.
unexpressiblePatterns.cdk8sAdvantage: cdk8s could include arbitrary resources more naturally, but this is a deployment concern, not a chart concern.

unexpressiblePatterns.workaround: Deploy sealed secrets separately as they are managed by a different operator. The dotCMS chart does not need to manage them.
unexpressiblePatterns.cdk8sAdvantage: None — sealed secrets are a separate concern regardless of tooling.

complexityMetrics.verdict: The _helpers.tpl is within acceptable complexity bounds. 28 helper functions and 42 conditionals for a multi-environment, multi-cloud chart is reasonable. No cyclomatic explosion detected.

structuralVariations.note: Zero customers require structural variations beyond parameter differences. All 84 customers run the standard dotCMS StatefulSet with standard EFS volumes.
